response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and systems software developer with genuine performance engineering experience (low-latency DLang services, high-throughput data pipelines), but the role demands SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator architecture, GPU workload analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear anywhere in this resume. His career is centered on web, mobile, and backend product development, and he shows no exposure to deep-learning workloads, silicon performance modeling, or computer architecture. While he clears the raw years-of-experience bar (BS + 8+ years), the domain mismatch for a Principal Performance Architect position is near-total.
strengths: Real low-latency performance work at Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
C/C++ systems experience at National Instruments and hardware-adjacent projects (robotic arms, multi-soundcard audio synthesis)
Long, versatile engineering career demonstrating strong software craftsmanship, testing discipline, and self-direction
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, computer architecture, or hardware performance modeling experience whatsoever
No deep-learning, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator workload background — the core of the role
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, far from principal-level hardware architecture scope

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI/deep-learning or GPU workload expertise
Low-latency systems and C/C++ performance background
